title: ConformanceProjectionRequest
description: One Projector scenario on the behavioural-conformance wire (spec §6.3.1). A conformance runner reads a JSON ARRAY of requests on stdin and writes a JSON ARRAY of outcomes on stdout — one per request, IN THE SAME ORDER — then exits 0; array position is the only correlation, so ordering is load-bearing. For a projection request the runner folds `given` — a sequence of events — through the relevant Projector, starting from that Projector's initial view, and answers with the resulting view state as a ConformanceProjectionResponse. Each scenario folds against fresh state (scenarios never share view state). Every payload value is a wire scalar (§3.3): `boolean`, 64-bit signed `integer`, or `string`; a field a payload schema declares `number` or `date` (§3.2) travels AS A STRING here, because the wire has no native fractional-number or date type. Self-contained: no cross-file `$ref`; reusable shapes inline in `$defs`.

given.description: The events the Projector folds, in order, from its initial view. May be empty (the empty history — the Projector's initial view state). Each item is an EventRef.

scalar.description: A wire scalar (§3.3): the only value types that cross this wire. `boolean`, a 64-bit signed `integer`, or a `string`. A payload field declared `number` or `date` (§3.2) is carried here AS A STRING — the wire alphabet has no fractional-number or date type, so those cross as their string form.

eventRef.description: A reference to an event with its payload. EITHER a bare id string (shorthand for that event with the EMPTY payload) OR an object naming the `event` id and its `with` payload. A missing `with` is the empty payload. Payload values are wire scalars (§3.3).

oneOf.description: Bare event id — the event with an empty payload.

with.description: The event payload: field name → wire scalar. Absent means the empty payload.
